behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Key generation is a pure deterministic transformation — input parameters in, string key out. The failure mode (collision or non-determinism) lives entirely inside one function. No module handoff is required to expose it. REAL-INFRA: No OS, network, or subprocess involved. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing to mock; the function has no external dependencies. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because the failure (collision or inconsistency) cannot be caused by two modules interacting — it is contained within the key-generation algorithm itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call the key-generation function with the same query-parameter dict multiple times and assert identical output; then call it with a large set of distinct parameter dicts (varying types, orderings, edge-case values) and assert no two distinct inputs produce the same key.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: TTL computation is a deterministic lookup/calculation: given a data category and a config object, return a duration. The failure mode (wrong TTL returned) lives inside the computation function and cannot hide behind a module boundary. REAL-INFRA: No I/O; the config can be a plain in-process object. MOCK-HIDE: No external dependency whose mock could suppress a real failure. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because wiring the cache to a DB is irrelevant to whether the TTL formula or category dispatch is correct.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a freshness-rule configuration with known values for each data category. Call the TTL-computation function with each category and assert the exact expected duration. Also test an unrecognized category to verify the fallback/error behavior.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is incorrect strategy selection: given a memory-usage value and a threshold config, return the wrong strategy. That logic lives inside one decision function. Caching layers almost always track their own byte-count internally rather than querying the OS, so the memory value is injectable. REAL-INFRA: No real OS memory probe needed — the test can supply a synthetic usage value above or below threshold. If the implementation does query OS-level memory (e.g., /proc/meminfo), the level rises to System; but the behavior text does not require that. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing external whose mock would hide the strategy-switch bug. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered in case the implementation reads OS memory directly, but the behavior text ('configured threshold') is consistent with an internal counter, so Unit is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the strategy-selector with a known threshold. Inject a memory-usage value just below the threshold and assert LRU is selected. Inject a value at and above the threshold and assert LFU is selected. Also verify the strategy reverts to LRU when usage drops back below threshold (if the implementation supports that).

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode spans two modules: the DB-write path and the cache-invalidation path. A unit test of the invalidation function alone cannot catch a bug where the write path never triggers invalidation, or where the key-matching logic misidentifies affected rows. At least the cache module and its DB-change listener/adapter must be wired together. REAL-INFRA: If invalidation is triggered by application-level explicit calls after a write, an in-process fake DB (or a local embedded DB) reproduces the failure without real network PostgreSQL — Integration suffices. If the mechanism uses PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY or triggers, a real DB instance is required (System). The behavior text does not specify the mechanism, so Integration is the minimum; reviewers should upgrade to System if the implementation uses DB-level notification. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the cache at unit level hides whether the write path actually calls invalidate and whether the key-match logic covers all affected rows. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) Real network not strictly required if application-level invalidation; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multiple agentic steps; (d) no LLM stages.
behaviors.test_description: Seed the in-process cache with several entries whose keys correspond to rows in a test table. Execute a write (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) against the test table through the full application write path. Assert that all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows are gone, and entries for unaffected rows remain intact.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If the invalidation mechanism is DB-level (triggers, LISTEN/NOTIFY), upgrade to System and use a real PostgreSQL instance (e.g., via testcontainers).

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: LRU and LFU are self-contained data-structure algorithms. The failure mode (wrong entry evicted) is entirely inside the eviction data structure and selection logic — no external module handoff is needed to expose it. REAL-INFRA: No I/O; the cache can be instantiated in-process at a small fixed capacity. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing external to mock. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered because eviction interacts with memory-pressure detection (behavior 3), but that interaction is not what this behavior tests — it tests correctness of the eviction selection algorithm itself, which is self-contained.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the cache at a known small capacity under each policy (LRU and LFU). For LRU: insert entries and access a subset in a specific order; fill the cache to trigger eviction and assert the least-recently-accessed entry was removed. For LFU: insert entries and access a subset with varying frequencies; fill the cache to trigger eviction and assert the least-frequently-accessed entry was removed. Cover tie-breaking behavior explicitly.
